Source URL: https://www.sbdc.uh.edu/sbdc/Consulting1.asp

# SBDC Stage to Resource Crosswalk

## Phase 1: Ideation
Use Start Your Business, Business Advising, startup webinars/classes, and market research. Route to SCORE as a companion mentor if the user needs operator judgment or ongoing accountability.

## Phase 2: Validation
Use Business Advising, Market Research/Marketing Resources, SBDCNet/Vertical IQ links, and business plan/financial projection support.

## Phase 3: Formation
Use Business Advising, startup classes/webinars, FAQs on starting a business, and professional referrals for legal/tax issues. Do not make final entity or tax recommendations.

## Phase 4: Launch / Initial Operations
Use Start Your Business, Marketing, AI Business Resources, Business Specialists, QuickBooks/accounting, HR, compliance, and customer-acquisition resources.

## Phase 5: Mature Operations
Use Grow Your Business, Business Specialists, training, cybersecurity, disaster preparedness, operations, finance, HR, and management webinars.

## Phase 6: Growth
Use Grow Your Business, market expansion, capital access, operations, workforce/manufacturing support, international trade, government procurement readiness, and growth-related success stories.

## Phase 7: Exit / Transition
Use Business Advising; the SBDC can help frame planning, financials, and transition questions but the user should be referred to appropriate legal, tax, valuation, and financial professionals for final decisions.

## Recovery / Troubleshooting
Use Disaster Preparedness, Cybersecurity, Business Specialists, Business Advising, and SBA disaster resources where relevant.
